title: Camera Setup Failed
shortDescription: The Nest Cam could not complete the initial setup process through the Google Home app.
fullDescription:
The Setup Error occurs when the Nest Cam cannot complete the initial pairing and configuration process through the Google Home app. This error can appear at various stages — during Bluetooth pairing, WiFi configuration, or account linking.

Common causes include Bluetooth interference, the phone being too far from the camera, WiFi incompatibility (Nest Cams require 2.4 GHz for initial setup), or Google Home app issues. Newer Nest Cam models use the Google Home app exclusively, while older Nest Cam models may still use the original Nest app.

Ensuring your phone has Bluetooth enabled, being within close range of the camera, and having your WiFi credentials ready before starting setup prevents most issues.

causes.cause: Bluetooth pairing failure between phone and camera
WiFi network incompatibility (5 GHz only, WPA3)
Google Home app outdated or cache corrupted

symptoms: 'Setup Error' or 'Could not set up device' in Google Home app
Camera LED pulses but setup does not progress
Setup freezes at WiFi configuration step
QR code on camera cannot be scanned
App cannot find the camera during setup

fixSteps.title: Restart setup with Bluetooth enabled
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure Bluetooth is enabled on your phone. Stand within 3 feet of the Nest Cam. Open Google Home app, tap + (Add), then Set up device > New device.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset the camera
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the reset button on the camera for 10 seconds until the LED flashes. Wait 2 minutes for the camera to enter setup mode, then retry setup.

fixSteps.title: Ensure 2.4 GHz WiFi is available
fixSteps.instruction: Nest Cam initial setup requires 2.4 GHz WiFi. Check your router settings to ensure a 2.4 GHz network is broadcasted. Some routers combine bands — try separating them temporarily.

fixSteps.title: Clear Google Home app cache
fixSteps.instruction: On Android: Settings > Apps > Google Home > Storage > Clear Cache. On iOS: Delete and reinstall the Google Home app. Sign back in and retry setup.

fixSteps.title: Try a different phone
fixSteps.instruction: If setup consistently fails, try using a different smartphone. Some phone models have Bluetooth compatibility issues with certain Nest Cam models.

whenToCallPro: If setup fails after factory reset and trying multiple phones, contact Google Nest support. The camera may have a hardware defect.
videoSearchQuery: Nest Cam setup error Google Home fix

faq.q: Do I need the Nest app or Google Home app?
faq.a: Newer Nest Cam models (2021+) use the Google Home app exclusively. Older models may still use the Nest app but are being migrated to Google Home.

faq.q: Why cannot the app find my Nest Cam during setup?
faq.a: Ensure Bluetooth is enabled, you are within 3 feet of the camera, and the camera is in setup mode (LED pulsing). If using an Android, ensure location permission is granted to Google Home.

title: Camera Lost WiFi Connection
shortDescription: The Nest Cam has lost its connection to the WiFi network.
fullDescription:
Connection Lost indicates that the Nest Cam can no longer communicate with your WiFi network. This disrupts live streaming, event recording, and notifications. The camera will attempt to reconnect automatically, but if the WiFi issue persists, manual intervention is needed.

Common causes include router restarts, WiFi password changes, signal degradation due to distance or interference, and power outages. Outdoor Nest Cams are particularly susceptible to weather-related signal issues.

Nest Cam (battery) models will continue to record events locally during brief WiFi outages if the Nest Aware subscription includes local storage, but cloud features are unavailable until connectivity is restored.

causes.cause: WiFi router rebooted or lost power
WiFi signal too weak at camera location

symptoms: 'Connection Lost' or 'Offline' in Google Home app
Camera LED shows offline status (varies by model)
Live stream unavailable
No motion or person alerts
Event history shows gap in recordings

fixSteps.title: Check your WiFi network
fixSteps.instruction: Verify your WiFi is working by connecting another device. If the router is down, restart it by unplugging for 30 seconds and plugging back in.

fixSteps.title: Restart the Nest Cam
fixSteps.instruction: For wired cameras, unplug the power cable for 10 seconds and reconnect. For battery cameras, remove the battery, wait 10 seconds, and reinsert it.

fixSteps.title: Move the camera closer to the router
fixSteps.instruction: If possible, reduce the distance between the camera and router, or remove physical obstructions. Thick walls, metal objects, and microwaves can weaken WiFi signals.

fixSteps.title: Reconnect to WiFi
fixSteps.instruction: In the Google Home app, tap on the camera > Settings (gear icon) > WiFi > Forget this network > Set up new WiFi. Follow the prompts to reconnect.

fixSteps.title: Add a WiFi extender
fixSteps.instruction: Install a WiFi extender or mesh WiFi node between the router and the Nest Cam. Google Nest WiFi is designed to work seamlessly with Nest cameras.

whenToCallPro: If the camera cannot reconnect despite strong WiFi signal and correct credentials, the WiFi module may be failing. Contact Google Nest support.
videoSearchQuery: Nest Cam connection lost WiFi fix

faq.q: Does Nest Cam record when WiFi is down?
faq.a: Nest Cam (battery) can store some events locally during brief outages. Wired Nest Cams require WiFi for all recording. Cloud recording requires both WiFi and a Nest Aware subscription.

faq.q: How far can Nest Cam be from the router?
faq.a: It depends on the environment. In an open area, 30-50 feet is typical. Through walls, the effective range drops significantly. Use the Google Home app to check signal quality.

title: Camera Battery Low or Depleted
shortDescription: The Nest Cam battery is running low and needs to be charged.
fullDescription:
The Low Battery notification appears when the Nest Cam (battery) has drained to a level where it may soon stop functioning. Nest Cam battery life varies from 1 to 7 months depending on activity level, temperature, and settings.

High-traffic areas with frequent motion events drain the battery faster. Extreme cold temperatures also significantly reduce battery life. Using features like 24/7 continuous recording (wired power required) or high video quality settings increases power consumption.

The Google Home app provides battery percentage and estimated remaining time. Charging the camera to full takes approximately 5 hours via the included USB-C cable.

causes.cause: High activity area causing frequent recordings
High video quality settings draining battery faster
Battery degradation from age or heat exposure

symptoms: Low Battery notification in Google Home app
Battery icon shows red or critical level
Camera reduces recording frequency to conserve power
Camera goes offline when battery is fully depleted
Reduced video quality as battery saving measure

fixSteps.title: Charge the camera
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the Nest Cam from its mount. Connect the included USB-C cable to the charging port on the camera and plug into a USB power adapter (5V/2A minimum). Charge until the LED indicates full charge (approximately 5 hours).

fixSteps.title: Reduce motion detection sensitivity
fixSteps.instruction: In Google Home app, go to camera settings > Detection > Motion Sensitivity. Lower the sensitivity to reduce unnecessary recordings that drain the battery.

fixSteps.title: Set up activity zones
fixSteps.instruction: In camera settings, define specific activity zones to limit where motion is detected. This prevents events triggered by passing cars, swaying trees, or other irrelevant motion.

fixSteps.title: Consider wired power
fixSteps.instruction: For high-activity locations, use the Nest Cam weatherproof charging cable to provide continuous power. This eliminates battery concerns and enables 24/7 recording.

whenToCallPro: If the battery no longer holds a charge or drains abnormally fast (less than a week), the battery may be defective. Contact Google Nest support for warranty evaluation.
videoSearchQuery: Nest Cam battery life tips extend charging

faq.q: How long does Nest Cam battery last?
faq.a: Typical battery life is 1-7 months depending on activity, settings, and temperature. High-traffic areas may require monthly charging.

faq.q: Can I use Nest Cam while it is charging?
faq.a: Yes, the camera continues to function normally while charging. You can also leave it permanently connected to power.

faq.q: Does cold weather affect Nest Cam battery?
faq.a: Yes, lithium batteries perform poorly in cold temperatures. Below 32°F (0°C), battery life can be significantly reduced. The camera may need more frequent charging in winter.

title: Camera Is Offline and Unresponsive
shortDescription: The Nest Cam is offline and cannot be reached by the Google Home app or cloud services.
fullDescription:
Camera Offline means the Nest Cam is not communicating with Google's cloud servers. This could be due to a power loss, WiFi disconnection, depleted battery (for battery models), or a hardware failure.

When offline, the camera cannot stream live video, send notifications, or record events to the cloud. Battery models may have some local event storage, but it is only accessible once the camera comes back online.

The Google Home app will show the camera as offline with the last known status time. Identifying whether the issue is power-related or network-related is the first step in troubleshooting.

causes.cause: Power loss — unplugged or breaker tripped
WiFi network down or password changed

symptoms: 'Offline' badge on camera in Google Home app
Live View shows 'Camera is offline'
No motion or person notifications
Camera LED is off or shows no activity
Last event timestamp is hours or days old

fixSteps.title: Check power supply
fixSteps.instruction: For wired cameras: verify the power cable is connected and the outlet has power. Try a different outlet. For battery cameras: check battery level — charge if depleted.

fixSteps.title: Check WiFi and internet
fixSteps.instruction: Verify your WiFi is working and internet is available by testing with another device. Restart your router if needed.

fixSteps.title: Restart the camera
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the camera power for 10 seconds and reconnect (wired), or remove and reinsert the battery (battery model). Wait 2 minutes for the camera to boot.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset if unresponsive
fixSteps.instruction: If the camera does not come online after power cycling, perform a factory reset. Press and hold the reset button for 10 seconds. Reconfigure through Google Home app.

whenToCallPro: If the camera does not come online after verifying power and WiFi, and a factory reset does not help, the camera may have a hardware failure requiring replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Nest Cam offline not connecting fix

faq.q: Does Nest Cam record when offline?
faq.a: Battery models can store some events locally during brief outages. Wired models do not record when offline. Cloud recording requires both power and internet.

faq.q: How do I know if my Nest Cam has power?
faq.a: Check the camera LED. A solid or blinking light indicates power. No light means no power. For battery models, charge for at least 30 minutes before checking.

title: Video Stream Failed to Load
shortDescription: The live video stream from the Nest Cam cannot be loaded or keeps buffering.
fullDescription:
Stream Error indicates that the Google Home app cannot establish or maintain a live video stream from the Nest Cam. The camera may be online and recording events, but the live view stream fails to load or constantly buffers.

This is primarily a bandwidth and network quality issue. Nest Cam video streams require consistent upload bandwidth from the camera to Google's servers and download bandwidth from the servers to your phone. Poor WiFi signal at the camera, ISP bandwidth limitations, or network congestion can all cause streaming failures.

The issue can also be caused by the Google Home app needing an update, the phone's network connection being slow, or temporary Google server issues.

causes.cause: Insufficient WiFi bandwidth at camera location
Phone network connection slow (cellular or WiFi)

symptoms: Stream Error message when opening live view
Black screen with loading spinner
Video starts then freezes or drops out
Audio plays but no video
Very pixelated or low-quality stream

fixSteps.title: Check camera WiFi signal
fixSteps.instruction: In Google Home app, tap on the camera > Settings > Technical Info. Check the WiFi signal strength. If weak, consider repositioning the camera or adding a WiFi extender.

fixSteps.title: Restart the camera
fixSteps.instruction: Power cycle the camera by unplugging for 10 seconds and reconnecting. This refreshes the network connection and clears any streaming errors.

fixSteps.title: Reduce video quality
fixSteps.instruction: In camera settings, lower the video quality from High to Low. This reduces bandwidth requirements and may resolve streaming issues on slower networks.

fixSteps.title: Switch phone to WiFi or cellular
fixSteps.instruction: If viewing on WiFi, try switching to cellular data, or vice versa. One connection may perform better than the other.

fixSteps.title: Improve network bandwidth
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect other bandwidth-heavy devices (streaming TVs, downloads, gaming). Ensure your internet plan provides at least 5 Mbps upload for reliable camera streaming.

whenToCallPro: If streaming issues persist with good WiFi and internet, contact Google Nest support. The camera's video encoder may be malfunctioning.
videoSearchQuery: Nest Cam stream error live view not loading fix

faq.q: How much bandwidth does Nest Cam need?
faq.a: Nest Cam uses 1-4 Mbps upload depending on video quality settings. For reliable streaming and viewing, at least 5 Mbps upload and download is recommended.

faq.q: Why does Nest Cam stream work on WiFi but not cellular?
faq.a: Cellular data speeds vary greatly. If your cellular connection is slow or congested, the stream may fail. Try again when you have better cellular reception.

title: Night Vision Not Activating or Poor Quality
shortDescription: The Nest Cam's infrared night vision is not activating automatically or produces poor image quality.
fullDescription:
Night Vision Error occurs when the Nest Cam's infrared (IR) LEDs fail to activate in low-light conditions, or when the night vision image quality is unusually poor. Nest Cams use invisible infrared LEDs to illuminate the scene in darkness, producing a black-and-white image.

Common causes include the night vision setting being turned off in the app, an IR LED hardware failure, a nearby light source confusing the ambient light sensor, or a dirty camera lens blocking the IR light.

Reflective surfaces close to the camera (glass windows, white walls) can also cause IR light to bounce back into the lens, creating washed-out or glaring images.

causes.cause: Night vision disabled in camera settings
Nearby light source confusing the light sensor
IR reflection from nearby glass or white surface

symptoms: Camera shows black image at night
Night vision does not switch on automatically
Night vision image is washed out or has glare
Only part of the image is visible at night
Night vision LED indicator does not glow (some models)

fixSteps.title: Enable night vision in settings
fixSteps.instruction: In Google Home app, tap on the camera > Settings > Night Vision. Ensure it is set to Auto or On. Auto activates night vision based on ambient light levels.

fixSteps.title: Clean the camera lens
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the camera lens and the IR LED ring around it with a soft, dry microfiber cloth. Dirt, fingerprints, or spider webs can block IR light.

fixSteps.title: Remove nearby light sources
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure no bright lights (porch lights, streetlights) are shining directly at the camera. These can trick the light sensor into thinking it is daytime, preventing night vision activation.

fixSteps.title: Reposition to avoid IR reflection
fixSteps.instruction: If the camera is pointed at or through glass, the IR light will reflect back and wash out the image. Move the camera so it is not facing a window, mirror, or glossy surface.

fixSteps.title: Restart the camera
fixSteps.instruction: Power cycle the camera to reset the night vision system. Unplug for 10 seconds and reconnect. Wait for the camera to come online and test night vision.

whenToCallPro: If night vision does not activate after checking settings and cleaning the lens, the IR LEDs may have failed. Contact Google Nest support for repair or replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Nest Cam night vision not working fix

faq.q: Can Nest Cam see in complete darkness?
faq.a: Yes, using infrared LEDs. The IR illumination allows the camera to see up to 20-25 feet in complete darkness. The image will be black and white.

faq.q: Why is my Nest Cam night vision image washed out?
faq.a: This is usually caused by IR light reflecting off a nearby surface like a wall, window, or eave. Move the camera so it has a clear field of view without close reflective surfaces.

title: Person Detection Not Working Correctly
shortDescription: The Nest Cam's person detection feature is not identifying people or is generating false alerts.
fullDescription:
Person Detection Error covers issues where the Nest Cam's AI-powered person detection fails to identify people, misidentifies objects as people, or does not send person-specific notifications. Nest Cam uses on-device machine learning to distinguish people from other motion.

Person detection requires a Nest Aware subscription on some models. Without the subscription, the camera only detects general motion. Even with the subscription, detection accuracy depends on camera placement, lighting, and the size of the person in the frame.

False positives (animals, shadows, or swaying plants triggering person alerts) and false negatives (people not being detected) are common tuning issues that can be improved through proper camera positioning and sensitivity settings.

causes.cause: Camera positioned too far from detection area

symptoms: No person-specific notifications despite people in view
Motion alerts for non-person movement (cars, animals)
Person detection badge missing from events
App shows 'Motion detected' instead of 'Person detected'
Familiar face detection not working

fixSteps.title: Verify Nest Aware subscription
fixSteps.instruction: In Google Home app, go to Settings > Subscriptions to confirm Nest Aware is active. Person detection requires an active subscription on most models.

fixSteps.title: Enable person detection
fixSteps.instruction: In camera settings, go to Detection > Event Types. Ensure Person is enabled. Also check that notifications for Person events are turned on.

fixSteps.title: Optimize camera placement
fixSteps.instruction: Mount the camera 7-10 feet high, angled slightly downward. Ensure the detection area is within 20 feet. People should occupy at least 10% of the frame for reliable detection.

fixSteps.title: Set up activity zones
fixSteps.instruction: Define activity zones to focus detection on specific areas (walkways, doors) and exclude areas with frequent non-person motion (streets, trees).

fixSteps.title: Update camera firmware
fixSteps.instruction: In Google Home app, check for camera updates. Go to camera settings > Technical Info. Updates improve detection AI accuracy.

whenToCallPro: Person detection issues are almost always resolved through settings and placement adjustments. Contact Google Nest support only if detection never works despite correct setup.
videoSearchQuery: Nest Cam person detection not working fix

faq.q: Do I need Nest Aware for person detection?
faq.a: On newer Nest Cam (battery/wired) models, basic person detection is included free. On older Nest Cam and Nest Cam IQ models, it requires a Nest Aware subscription.

faq.q: How far can Nest Cam detect a person?
faq.a: Reliable person detection works up to about 20 feet from the camera. Beyond that, the person may be too small in the frame for the AI to identify them.

title: Events Not Being Recorded or Saved
shortDescription: The Nest Cam is not recording events or video clips are missing from the history.
fullDescription:
Recording Error indicates that the Nest Cam is not saving motion events, person detection events, or continuous video to the cloud. This can manifest as missing events in the timeline, gaps in continuous recording, or no event history at all.

Recording requires an active internet connection, sufficient cloud storage (via Nest Aware subscription), and the camera being online and powered. Battery-powered models may skip recordings to conserve battery when the charge is low.

Nest Aware subscribers get event video history (up to 60 days depending on plan) and optional 24/7 continuous recording (wired cameras only). Without a subscription, recording features are limited.

causes.cause: Nest Aware subscription expired or not active
Camera battery too low to record (battery models)

symptoms: No events in video history despite motion
Gaps in the event timeline
'No recent activity' despite known events
Events shown as thumbnails but no video clip
Continuous recording timeline has missing segments

fixSteps.title: Check Nest Aware subscription
fixSteps.instruction: In Google Home app > Settings > Subscriptions. Verify Nest Aware is active and your payment method is current. Expired subscriptions stop cloud recording.

fixSteps.title: Verify detection is enabled
fixSteps.instruction: Go to camera settings > Detection. Ensure Motion detection and Person detection are enabled. Check that the sensitivity is not set too low.

fixSteps.title: Check internet connectivity
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the camera is online in Google Home app. Check camera WiFi signal strength. A weak or intermittent connection causes recording gaps.

fixSteps.title: Charge battery or connect to power
fixSteps.instruction: For battery models, charge to at least 20%. Below this level, the camera may reduce or stop recording to preserve battery for critical alerts.

fixSteps.title: Restart the camera
fixSteps.instruction: Power cycle the camera to clear any recording system errors. This often resolves temporary recording issues.

whenToCallPro: If recordings are missing despite active subscription, good WiFi, and correct settings, contact Google Nest support. There may be a cloud-side issue with your account.
videoSearchQuery: Nest Cam not recording events fix

faq.q: How long does Nest Cam keep recordings?
faq.a: Without Nest Aware: 3 hours of event snapshots. Nest Aware basic: 30 days of event history. Nest Aware Plus: 60 days of event history plus 10 days of 24/7 recording (wired only).

faq.q: Why are there gaps in my Nest Cam recording?
faq.a: Gaps are usually caused by WiFi disconnections, power interruptions, or the camera conserving battery. Check Device Health in the app for WiFi signal quality.

title: Google Home App Error When Accessing Camera
shortDescription: The Google Home app encounters an error when trying to access or control the Nest Cam.
fullDescription:
App Error refers to issues within the Google Home (or legacy Nest) app that prevent users from accessing their Nest Cam feeds, settings, or event history. The camera itself may be functioning normally, but the app cannot display or control it.

Common causes include an outdated app version, corrupted app cache, Google account authentication issues, or compatibility problems with the phone's operating system version. Google frequently updates the Home app, and older versions may lose compatibility.

Force closing and reopening the app, clearing the cache, or reinstalling the app resolves most app-related errors.
symptoms: Error message when opening camera in Google Home
App crashes when tapping on camera feed
Camera tile shows loading spinner indefinitely
'Something went wrong' message
Settings pages fail to load

fixSteps.title: Force close and reopen the app
fixSteps.instruction: Swipe the Google Home app away from recent apps (iOS: swipe up, Android: swipe away). Reopen the app and try accessing the camera.

fixSteps.title: Update the Google Home app
fixSteps.instruction: Open the App Store (iOS) or Google Play Store (Android) and check for Google Home app updates. Install any available updates.

fixSteps.title: Clear app cache
fixSteps.instruction: Android: Settings > Apps > Google Home > Storage > Clear Cache. iOS: Delete and reinstall the app (iOS does not allow manual cache clearing).

fixSteps.title: Re-sign into your Google account
fixSteps.instruction: In the Google Home app, sign out and sign back in. This refreshes your authentication token and resolves account-linked access issues.

fixSteps.title: Check for Google service outages
fixSteps.instruction: Visit status.cloud.google.com or check DownDetector for Google Home or Nest outages. If there is a service disruption, wait for resolution.

whenToCallPro: If the app consistently fails with an updated version and cleared cache, contact Google Nest support. The issue may be account-specific or require server-side resolution.
videoSearchQuery: Google Home app Nest Cam error not loading fix

faq.q: Should I use the Nest app or Google Home app?
faq.a: Google is migrating all Nest devices to the Google Home app. Newer cameras only work with Google Home. If you have older devices, you may be prompted to migrate.

faq.q: What if the app works on one phone but not another?
faq.a: Ensure both phones have the latest Google Home app and OS version. Some older phones may not be compatible with newer app versions.

title: Nest Aware Subscription Issue
shortDescription: There is a problem with the Nest Aware subscription affecting recording and detection features.
fullDescription:
Subscription Error indicates an issue with the Nest Aware subscription linked to your Google account. This can prevent cloud recording, person detection, familiar face recognition, and extended video history from working.

Nest Aware subscriptions are managed through the Google Store and billed through your Google account. Issues arise when payment methods expire, billing fails, the subscription was accidentally canceled, or when migrating from the legacy Nest subscription to the Google-managed Nest Aware plan.

Without an active Nest Aware subscription, Nest Cam functionality is reduced to basic motion detection, live view, and limited event snapshots (3 hours on newer models).

causes.cause: Payment method expired or billing failed
Migration from legacy Nest to Google billing incomplete
Google account issue preventing subscription verification

symptoms: Person detection stops working
Video history limited to 3 hours
24/7 continuous recording stops
'Upgrade to Nest Aware' prompts appear
Familiar face recognition disabled
Activity zones no longer available

fixSteps.title: Check subscription status
fixSteps.instruction: Open Google Home app > Settings > Subscriptions. Verify Nest Aware shows as Active. If expired, follow prompts to resubscribe.

fixSteps.title: Update payment method
fixSteps.instruction: Go to pay.google.com and check your payment methods. Update any expired credit cards or add a new payment method. Google will retry billing.

fixSteps.title: Resubscribe to Nest Aware
fixSteps.instruction: If the subscription lapsed, go to Google Home app > Settings > Subscriptions > Subscribe. Choose your plan (Nest Aware at $8/month or Nest Aware Plus at $15/month in the US).

fixSteps.title: Complete legacy Nest migration
fixSteps.instruction: If you had an older Nest subscription, you may need to migrate to the Google-managed plan. Go to home.nest.com > Settings > Nest Aware and follow the migration prompts.

fixSteps.title: Contact Google support for billing issues
fixSteps.instruction: If the subscription shows active but features are not working, or if you have billing disputes, contact Google Nest support for account-level troubleshooting.

whenToCallPro: Contact Google Nest support if billing issues persist or if legacy subscription migration is not completing properly.
videoSearchQuery: Nest Aware subscription not working fix

faq.q: What does Nest Aware cost?
faq.a: In the US, Nest Aware is approximately $8/month (30-day event history) or Nest Aware Plus is approximately $15/month (60-day event history plus 10-day continuous recording). Prices vary by region.

faq.q: What features work without Nest Aware?
faq.a: Without Nest Aware, you get live view, basic motion notifications, and 3 hours of event snapshots on newer models. Person detection, extended history, and continuous recording require Nest Aware.

faq.q: Can I share Nest Aware across multiple cameras?
faq.a: Yes, one Nest Aware subscription covers all Nest cameras in your home (same Google Home/house). You do not need a separate subscription per camera.
